TEST.0.title: Codebusters 2026 Division B Regional Test # 12
TEST.0.customHeader:
Names of participants: (Please print neatly)

 
 
 
School Name:

Check your team:  ◻ Varsity     ◻ JV1     ◻ JV2     ◻ JV3

Warning: Do not open this packet until given permission to do so.

Note: There are useful notes after this page.

CIPHER.565.cipherString: Before every one stands an image of what he ought to be; so long as he is not that, his peace is not complete.
CIPHER.565.question: The following quote by Rückert needs to be decoded with the Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.565.author: Rückert

CIPHER.547.cipherString: Life is about overcoming obstacles and they are all mental
CIPHER.547.question: You intercepted this transmission from a rogue weather balloon. Decode the Baconian version of Shaun S. Lott's famous words. What does it say?
CIPHER.547.author: Shaun S. Lott

CIPHER.562.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Affine-encrypted quote by Alfred North Whitehead with a=11 and b=6. What does it say?
CIPHER.562.author: Alfred North Whitehead

CIPHER.541.cipherString: Bunsen was no theorist, and purely speculative questions had little or no interest for him.
CIPHER.541.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote, encrypted as an Aristocrat. What does it say?

CIPHER.564.cipherString: I put a new freezer next to the refrigerator, now they're just chilling.
CIPHER.564.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote using the Checkerboard Cipher with a polybius key of JOURNALIST. What does it say?
CIPHER.564.author: Unknown

CIPHER.549.cipherString: Only the most uncritical minds are free from doubt.
CIPHER.549.question: While analyzing the background noise from a physics experiment, you detect this pattern. It's a quote by Aldo Leopold, encoded using a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote has SAREF in it corresponding to the encoded text E Y K D A X N, meaning A = ●××; D = –●×; E = ●●●; K = –×●; N = ●×●; X = ●●–; Y = ××●. What does it say?
CIPHER.549.author: Aldo Leopold

CIPHER.555.cipherString: He will win who knows how to handle both superior and inferior forces.
CIPHER.555.question: Hidden in the source code of a retro video game, this Complete Columnar puzzle reveals a quote from Total War: Shogun 2. The quote has RFOR somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.555.author: Total War: Shogun 2

CIPHER.558.cipherString: When I don't understand, I have an unbearable itch to know why.
CIPHER.558.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Nihilist Substitution-encrypted message by Robert A. Heinlein with a keyword of TRIPLE and polybius key of FACTORIES. What does it say?
CIPHER.558.author: Robert A. Heinlein

CIPHER.542.cipherString: Pain reaches the heart with electrical speed, but truth moves to the heart as slowly as a glacier.
CIPHER.542.question: Someone left a paper with the message below on the copy machine in the office. It's a quote by Barbara Kingsolver, encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.542.author: Barbara Kingsolver

CIPHER.552.question: Your microscope slide prep strangely revealed this Cryptarithm message. What do the values 4622863 723512 9503 decode to?

CIPHER.545.cipherString: Todos tenemos el poder de tomar pequeñas decisiones que pueden cambiar el curso de nuestras vidas para siempre.
CIPHER.545.question: A quote by Oscar Auliq-Ice in Spanish has been encoded using the Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet for you to decode. You are told that the cipher starts with TODOS. What does it say?
CIPHER.545.author: Oscar Auliq-Ice
CIPHER.545.translation: We all have the power to make little choices that can change the course of our lives forever.

CIPHER.550.cipherString: Life is too short. I don't have time to speak slowly.
CIPHER.550.question: A glitchy hologram in the school planetarium displays this Fractionated Morse version of Dev Anand's words. You are told that the quote has THAVET in it corresponding to the encoded text Z Y D R Q I A K, meaning A = ●××; D = ●●●; I = ●–×; K = –×●; Q = ×●●; R = ×●–; Y = ××●; Z = ××–. What does it say?
CIPHER.550.author: Dev Anand

CIPHER.560.cipherString: Courts do not exist for the sake of discipline, but for the sake of deciding matters in controversy.
CIPHER.560.question: The marching band's sheet music rearranged itself into this Caesar pattern—it's a quote by Charles Bowen, Baron Bowen encoded. What does it say?
CIPHER.560.author: Charles Bowen, Baron Bowen

CIPHER.557.cipherString: Education enables people and societies to be what they can be.
CIPHER.557.question: Your smart fridge started flashing this cryptic message at 3 a.m. It's by Bill Richardson encoded in a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of GLORY and polybius key of GOLDFISH. What does it say?
CIPHER.557.author: Bill Richardson

CIPHER.548.cipherString: He who cannot do as he would, must do as he can.
CIPHER.548.question: Your smartwatch notification contained an Italian Proverb in a Baconian cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.548.author: Italian Proverb

CIPHER.544.cipherString: There is nothing more beautiful than cheerfulness in an old face, and among country people it is always a sign of a well-regulated life.
CIPHER.544.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Richter encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher ends with LIFE. What does it say?
CIPHER.544.author: Richter

CIPHER.559.cipherString: Identify a challenge in your area and see how best you can help.
CIPHER.559.question: Astronomers notice this repeating sequence in deep-space data. It's actually a quote by Lillian Nkosazana Moremi, encoded with an Atbash. What does it say?
CIPHER.559.author: Lillian Nkosazana Moremi

CIPHER.543.cipherString: Long ago I discovered that there was more to life than cricket, and more to cricket than runs and wickets.
CIPHER.543.question: David Foot offers us some advice that has been encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with LONG. What does it say?
CIPHER.543.author: David Foot

CIPHER.556.cipherString: It's always such a joy that you wake up in the morning and there's work to do.
CIPHER.556.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Jerome Lawrence, written in a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has KTODO somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.556.author: Jerome Lawrence

CIPHER.553.cipherString: There is always room for improvement, always. Everything can be improved.
CIPHER.553.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Mapi León encoded as a Porta using a keyword of RUBY. What does it say?
CIPHER.553.author: Mapi León

CIPHER.551.question: Your lab partner discovered this pattern on the back of a graphing calculator. It's a Cryptarithm. What do the values 80014558 72457 92316 decode to?

CIPHER.563.cipherString: If your experiment needs a statistician, you need a better experiment.
CIPHER.563.question: While scrolling through an old floppy disk labeled "TOP SECRET", you discovered this quote by Ernest Rutherford hidden as a Checkerboard with a polybius key of SPARKLING. What does it say?
CIPHER.563.author: Ernest Rutherford

CIPHER.561.cipherString: Knowledge is the food of the soul.
CIPHER.561.question: The message below was encoded into the DNA sequence of a synthetic organism. It translates to a quote by Plato, encrypted using an Affine Cipher with a=21 and b=23. What does it say?
CIPHER.561.author: Plato

CIPHER.554.cipherString: Acting is magical. Change your look and your attitude, and you can be anyone.
CIPHER.554.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by Alicia Witt, written in a Porta using a keyword of BRICK. What does it say?
CIPHER.554.author: Alicia Witt

CIPHER.546.cipherString: It is delivery that makes the orator's success.
CIPHER.546.question: A laser-etched message on the inside of a geodes crystal reads: solve this Baconian version of Goethe's wisdom. You are told that the deciphered text starts with ITIS. What does it say?
CIPHER.546.author: Goethe
